content: The human language learning practice of structured reflexion (reflection) on writing assignments can be directly adapted to improve LLM self-reflection mechanisms, by framing LLM self-correction like a teacher-guided high school writing reflection workshop. This approach treats LLM output iteration not just as a technical debugging step, but as a scaffolded learning process that builds long-term agent memory for better problem-solving, mirroring how human students improve through repeated structured reflection on their own work.
